title: UPS Battery Health
dataSources: Poll standard UPS-MIB battery variables through Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266) or the SNMP Modular Input add-on (Splunkbase app ID 1537) documented for SNMP TA (UPS-MIB) deployments; normalize events into index=power with sourcetype=snmp:ups carrying extracted fields battery_replace_indicator, charge_pct, runtime_min, ups_name, location, battery_status, battery_age_months, and host as management identity.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where battery_replace_indicator="yes" OR charge_pct < 80 OR runtime_min < 15
| table ups_name, location, battery_status, charge_pct, runtime_min, battery_age_months
description: Monitors SNMP UPS-MIB battery-health telemetry—replacement-warning semantics, charge percentage, projected runtime at load, cabinet temperature context, and aging counters—so Splunk surfaces racks whose UPS strings can no longer ride through utility disturbances.
value: Facility and platform owners get measurable runway to schedule controlled replacements before protected cabinets brown out or corrupt storage during transfers; fewer surprise outages shorten incident bridges and preserve orderly shutdown orchestration when mains quality falters.
implementation: Establish five-minute SNMP walks (or equivalent Splunk Connect for SNMP profiles) targeting each UPS management endpoint for UPS-MIB battery OIDs; align Heavy Forwarder or worker clocks to NTP before interpreting runtime deltas. Threshold the saved search when battery_replace_indicator asserts replacement, charge_pct stays below policy for consecutive polls, or runtime_min violates minimum runway while honoring rack-level suppression lookups during planned maintenance.
visualization: Primary dashboard row: dual-axis timechart overlaying runtime_min and charge_pct split by ups_name with fifteen-minute spans for smoothing; companion statistic panels for fleet minimum runtime and count of UPS assets breaching thresholds; detail drilldown table sorted ascending on runtime_min with color ranges for battery_status; optional choropleth or rack-floor SVG only when CMDB supplies coordinates.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable — SNMP UPS telemetry is not routed through Splunk CIM data models for this observability UC; acceleration applies only if you voluntarily normalize metrics into custom indexes via summary indexing.

references.title: Splunk Connect for SNMP — Splunkbase listing

knownFalsePositives:
• Scheduled UPS battery self-test initiated from the UPS embedded Web UI—runtime_min collapses for one poll though battery_status moves to testing; suppress via CMDB maintenance tags covering the five-minute SNMP windows tied to ups_name.
• Demand-response load shedding coordinated through building EMS—fleet-wide charge_pct dips simultaneously without hardware faults; distinguish using EMS curtailed-load annotations versus SNMP-only anomalies.
• DR drills where operators intentionally drain strings under supervised scripts—battery_replace_indicator may latch yes briefly; suppress using drill calendars keyed by datacenter facility codes.
• Heavy Forwarder SNMP worker restart storms—every ups_name misses polls with identical splunk_server gaps while interactive snmpwalk still returns healthy UPS-MIB counters; throttle alerts until modular input heartbeat restores.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install SNMP TA (UPS-MIB) collection via Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266) **or** SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1537) on a Heavy Forwarder that routes UDP/161 to each UPS management interface; stash SNMPv3 authPriv secrets outside repo-tracked configs.
• Reserve index=power (or your contracted SNMP landing index) and confirm props/transforms map inbound traps/walks to sourcetype=snmp:ups with FIELDALIAS/EVAL coverage for battery_replace_indicator, charge_pct, runtime_min, ups_name, location, battery_status, and battery_age_months exactly as referenced in this UC's SPL.
• Grant operators a scoped Splunk RBAC role (for example power_ops) limited to index=power plus dependent lookups/macros.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Author modular-input or SC4SNMP profiles that poll UPS-MIB battery tables every five minutes; document OID-to-field parity in internal runbooks because firmware builds stringify enumerated columns differently under snmpwalk.
• Bind destinations to management IPs/VLANs cleared by firewall change tickets and validate throughput stays under license projections (~few kilobytes per poll cycle per UPS).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the SPL bundled with this UC inside Search, save as a report for trending, then clone into a scheduled alert with cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *, dispatch.earliest_time=-15m, dispatch.latest_time=now to span at least three SNMP samples.

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where battery_replace_indicator="yes" OR charge_pct < 80 OR runtime_min < 15
| table ups_name, location, battery_status, charge_pct, runtime_min, battery_age_months
```

Understanding this SPL
• Base scope pins index=power and sourcetype=snmp:ups—rename only when your ingestion contract differs.
• Filtering isolates actionable degradation rows via battery_replace_indicator, charge_pct, or runtime_min thresholds tuned per rack criticality.
• Tabular output prioritizes ops review columns ups_name through battery_age_months for CMDB reconciliation.

Step 3 — Validate
Verify charge_pct and runtime_min against the UPS embedded Web UI / LCD summary for the same management IP during pilot polling; reconcile discrepancies by capturing simultaneous snmpget output before widening alerting.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish dashboards/alerts referenced under Visualization and attach webhook/PagerDuty actions per severity matrix.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• Symptom: modular input timeout bursts yet snmpwalk from a bastion succeeds—inspect UDP/161 ACL drift on upstream switches or SNMP Engine ID churn after credential rotations.
• Symptom: no snmp:ups events for newly commissioned racks—confirm inputs.conf stanza renamed indexes match outputs used by Deployment Server classes.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Lab UPS emits SNMP polling samples on sourcetype=snmp:ups where battery_replace_indicator=yes across three sequential five-minute polls while snmpwalk confirms the same replacement OID asserts true and runtime_min stays below policy.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Heavy Forwarder SNMP modular input loses UDP reachability during a router reload so Splunk drops snmp:ups samples even though engineers still snmpwalk healthy UPS-MIB counters from their laptops—suppress paging once collector faults are isolated.

evidence: Archived PDF or screenshot bundle of the saved UPS Battery Health alert alongside indexed snmp:ups rows in index=power for the audited quarter, paired with SNMP capture proving correlated UPS-MIB readings.
exclusions: Does not cover unmanaged UPS hardware lacking SNMP endpoints, proprietary MIB variants whose counters lack parity with UPS-MIB semantics, or predictive cell chemistry modeling beyond polled SNMP gauges referenced here.
